sha_convention: bytes on disk as-is (binary read, no line-ending normalization)

ac1.description: fp6_provenance.classify: sampler BASE+ADAPTER_PATH under adapters/ resolves via BASE; basis sampler-stamp-with-own-adapter:<base>+<adapter-tag>; unknown bases still UNKNOWN
ac1.path_normalization: Windows B:/... and /mnt/b/... paths both recognized via _canon_path()

ac2.description: ledger_license.effective_class: STAMPED UNKNOWN with sampler-unmapped: basis re-classifies on read; stamped non-UNKNOWN wins
ac2.branch_A: stamped UNKNOWN + sampler-unmapped: -> re-classify via classify() -> qwen-research

ac3.description: Fresh license sidecar backfill: 960 r2 rows_read (278 ep + 98 ctl added to ledger after frontier cap) -> qwen-research via AC1+AC2

ac4.description: ledger_dedup fp16 crosscheck: PIN_STALE when sha differs; MISMATCH only on same-snapshot compare
ac4.discrepancy_from_issue_estimate: Issue text 592->585/697->692 was speculative; actual: 725/745 due to r2 adding 140/53 pure-new clusters beyond the 48/22 bridging rows. 48 bridge clusters reduce episode count by 7 (732 without bridging -> 725). Documented in ledger_dedup.py fp16_ground_truth.growth_note.

ac5.description: Main ledger files byte-untouched throughout all AC3+AC4 operations
